whatIs.title: An Ancient Curse, Alive Online
whatIs.description: For 300 years, people have gathered beneath Hong Kong bridges to strike paper effigies and curse their enemies. Now this ritual is online — free, dark, and waiting. Strike. Burn. What if it works?
whatIs.linkText: Learn the full history of this ritual →

footer.heritage: Based on authentic Cantonese folk traditions. We work to preserve and share this cultural heritage.

about.title: About BeatPetty
about.subtitle: Preserving a 300-year-old Hong Kong tradition for the digital age.
about.storyP1: BeatPetty was born from a moment beneath the Goose Neck Bridge in Hong Kong's Wan Chai district. Every spring, on Jingzhe (驚蟄), hundreds gather under the overpass to beat paper effigies with old shoes — cursing those who wronged them. The air fills with smoke, chanting, and a strange kind of hope.
about.storyP2: This is 打小人 (Da Siu Yan) — "Beating the Petty Person." A Cantonese folk tradition dating back over 300 years, recognized as part of Hong Kong's intangible cultural heritage. Not superstition. Not a game. A genuine ritual that real people have trusted for generations.
about.storyP3: We created BeatPetty to bring this ritual to the world. For those who can't travel to Hong Kong. For those who need closure that logic can't provide. For those willing to ask: what if it works?
about.missionText: To preserve and share the 打小人 tradition in its authentic form — dark, atmospheric, and unapologetically supernatural. We believe cultural heritage deserves a digital home that respects its origins.

items.question: What is 打小人 (Da Siu Yan)?
items.answer: 打小人 — literally 'beating the petty person' — is a Cantonese curse ritual where practitioners strike paper effigies to curse their enemies. Originating in Guangdong province and practiced at Hong Kong's Goose Neck Bridge, it is recognized as part of Hong Kong's intangible cultural heritage.

items.question: Is the curse ritual free?
items.answer: The basic curse ritual is completely free — five of the eight traditional steps: invocation, declaration, fire pass, beating, and burning. Optional enhancements — the personalized curse reading or the completion ritual (purification, blessing, divination) — are available for a small fee.

items.question: What happens during the curse ritual?
items.answer: The ritual follows the traditional 八部曲 (eightfold ritual) practiced at Hong Kong's Goose Neck Bridge. You invoke the spirits, name your enemy, pass the paper over fire, strike the effigy, and burn it to ash. The full experience adds purification, blessing, and a final divination with moon blocks — about two minutes total.

items.question: Does the curse ritual actually work?
items.answer: This is a centuries-old tradition practiced by millions across generations. Whether the curse takes hold is between you and the ritual. The emotional release is real. The tradition is ancient. What if it works?

items.question: Can I curse a specific person by name?
items.answer: Yes. The enhanced ritual lets you speak your enemy's true name into the ceremony. The name appears on the paper effigy and burns with it. This makes the curse personal.

openings: The curse has found its mark. {target}, the betrayer, will not escape what has been set in motion.
The ritual energy has locked onto {target}. Their shadow has been claimed.
The ancient words have been spoken. {target}'s fate is now sealed in fire.
impacts: Their deceptions will turn against them. By the next moon, those they betrayed will see the truth.
Every lie they speak will cost them an ally. The curse feeds on their greatest weakness — their tongue.
timings: The first signs will appear within three days. A sudden change in their demeanor. A crack in their composure.
By the seventh day, the curse reaches full strength. {target} will not understand what is happening — only that things have shifted.
The effects build slowly, like rust. By the next new moon, the damage will be irreversible.
weaknesses: Their greatest weakness is trust — they depend on others believing their lies. The curse erodes that belief.
Their vulnerability lies in their need to be liked. When the mask slips, the audience leaves.
They fear exposure above all else. The curse targets the gap between their public face and private actions.
closings: This is not a warning. This is a verdict.
The curse has been spoken. It cannot be unspoken.
What is done cannot be undone. The fire has consumed. The ash has settled.

openings: The curse descends upon {target}. Their authority means nothing here.
The ritual has named {target}. Power without wisdom invites its own destruction.
{target}'s reign of cruelty has been witnessed. The spirits remember.
impacts: Those they crushed will rise. Their influence will crumble from within.
The fear they planted in others will bloom in their own chest. Sleep will not come easily.
timings: Within five days, their authority will be questioned by someone they never expected.
The curse works from the bottom up. Subordinates first, then peers, then superiors. Full effect in ten days.
weaknesses: Their power is borrowed, not earned. The curse dissolves the illusion that holds it together.
They rely on fear as currency. When fear fades — and it will — they have nothing left.
Their weakness is the fragile ego behind the title. The curse knows exactly where to press.
closings: The curse does not negotiate. Neither did they.
What they built on fear will collapse. The foundation was always hollow.
The ritual is complete. Their power was never real.

openings: The curse reaches through closed doors. {target} is not beyond its grasp.
{target} should have stayed in the past. The ritual ensures they will.
The flame that burns for {target} is not love. It is reckoning.
impacts: The memories they left behind will haunt them in reverse. What they took will be returned — doubled.
Their new foundations will crack. What they built on betrayal cannot stand.
Every door they closed will lock from the other side. They will knock. No one will answer.
timings: Old wounds will reopen for them within the week. Not yours — theirs.
By the tenth day, {target} will feel the weight of every door they closed.
The timing is precise. The next time they think of you, the curse activates. That moment is closer than they think.
weaknesses: Their weakness is the belief that they moved on unscathed. They haven't. The curse simply reveals what was already broken.
They fear being forgotten more than being hated. The curse ensures they remember exactly what they lost.
Their vulnerability is the narrative they tell themselves — 'I made the right choice.' The curse rewrites that story.
closings: Some doors should stay closed. The curse agrees.
This chapter is finished. The book has been burned.
The ritual has spoken. {target} is the past. The past stays buried.

openings: The curse has identified {target}. The drain ends now.
{target} has fed long enough. The ritual cuts the cord they never deserved.
The spirits see {target} for what they are — a parasite in human form.
impacts: The energy they stole will sour in their possession. What nourished them will poison them.
The void they carry will consume them from within. They will drain themselves dry.
timings: Within three days, the cord between you and {target} will begin to dissolve. You will feel lighter.
The isolation begins this week. One by one, the people around {target} will start pulling away.
The curse feeds on their emptiness. By day seven, they will drain themselves for the first time.
weaknesses: Their weakness is their inability to be alone. Without others to feed on, they starve.
They have no inner reserves. The curse cuts the supply line they've been tapping.
Their greatest fear is insignificance. Without energy to steal, they become exactly that — invisible.
closings: The cord is cut. {target} feeds on nothing now.
The parasite has been named. The host is free.
The ritual is complete. Their hunger will be their ruin.

openings: The curse confronts {target}. Their cruelty has been recorded.
{target} ruled through fear. The ritual answers with something older.
The spirits have weighed {target} and found them wanting.
impacts: The fear they planted will harvest in their own fields. They will know how it feels.
Their strength will become their prison. The walls they built for others will close in on them.
Those who stood silent will find their voice. {target} will not recognize the world they wake up in.
timings: Within the week, someone will stand up to them. The curse makes the first move.
The power shift begins in five days. Watch for a public moment where their words carry no weight.
Their reign ends not with a dramatic fall, but with a quiet erosion. It has already begun.
weaknesses: Their weakness is the fear they project. Behind it is a coward who cannot survive being challenged.
They rely on the silence of others. The curse gives voice to the quiet ones.
Their vulnerability is the thin skin beneath the aggression. The curse will find it.
closings: The table has turned. {target} just doesn't know it yet.
The ritual does not forget. Neither do the broken.

openings: The curse has been aimed. {target} — you know exactly who they are. So does the ritual now.
The name {target} has been spoken into the fire. It cannot be unspoken.
impacts: Their plans will unravel. Their certainties will become questions. Their ground will shift beneath their feet.
The universe has been nudged. Coincidences will stack against them. Not dramatic — just relentless.
The curse works in silence. {target} will not see it coming. But they will feel it.
timings: The curse takes effect within three to seven days. Watch for small, unexplained shifts in {target}'s world.
There is no precise timeline — only inevitability. What the ritual set in motion cannot be stopped.
The effects will be subtle at first. A coincidence here, a setback there. Then they compound.
weaknesses: Their vulnerability is the thing they protect most carefully. The curse goes straight for it.
Their blind spot is the gap between how they see themselves and how the world sees them. The curse widens that gap.
closings: You named them. The ritual heard. What happens next is between them and the curse.
The paper has burned. The ash has scattered. The curse is loose.
This is between you, {target}, and the ancient ones. The ritual is done.

insights: This one did not appear by chance. The ritual has revealed a pattern: you see the best in people, and some exploit that gift.
The betrayal was not about you. But your instinct to trust without verifying — that is what the spirits marked.
There is a door inside you that stays unlocked. This person walked through it. The ritual asks: who else has the key?
resolutions: The ritual reveals a pattern in your trust. Next time, let them prove their words before you carry them. The spirits do not rush — neither should your faith.
The oracle speaks: before you open the next door, look through the keyhole first. Not everyone who knocks should be let in.
Close the door, then lock it. Not everyone deserves a second chance to prove they didn't deserve the first.
prophecies: After this ritual, your instincts will sharpen. The next betrayer will be visible before they strike.
The wound this person left will become a scar, and that scar will become armor. You are not weakened — you are being forged.
The people who remain after this ritual are the ones forged in the same fire. The rest were never rooted — they were just passing through.

insights: Their power over you is partly borrowed — you handed it to them through doubt. The ritual has seen this.
You have been waiting for permission that was never theirs to give. The spirits recognize your restraint as strength misapplied.
The authority they hold is real. But the authority they hold over your peace — that was always yours to revoke.
resolutions: Your value is not determined by someone who cannot see it. The ritual advises: know your exit, even if you never use it.
Do not fight them on their terms. Fight by becoming impossible to replace, then leaving.
The curse weakens them. You strengthen yourself. Both can be true at once.
prophecies: A professional shift approaches. Within one cycle, the ground beneath them will shift — and you will already be standing elsewhere.
What you endured here will become the standard by which you measure every future opportunity. You will not settle again.
The ledger is not balanced yet. But the ritual has added your name to the other side of the scale. What was taken from you will return — not as a gift, but as something earned.

insights: What you are holding onto is not them — it is who you were when you were with them. The ritual sees the difference.
The door they closed was never the problem. It is the window you keep opening that lets the cold in.
This person occupies a space in your story that was never meant to be permanent. The spirits confirm: the chapter ended long ago.
resolutions: Close every door. Not out of anger — out of clarity. Some windows exist to show you what not to return to.
The ritual advises: do not seek closure from the one who opened the wound. Seek it from within.
Redirect the energy. What you have spent remembering them, spend on remembering yourself.
prophecies: When you stop looking back, you will notice someone has been waiting for you to face forward.
The version of you that survives this will be unrecognizable — in the best possible way.
The ritual foresees: the next time you think of this person, you will feel nothing. That day is closer than you think.

insights: Your energy is a resource more precious than money. This person has been spending it freely — and you let them.
The ritual has identified the drain: it is not dramatic. It is constant, quiet, and invisible — until you are empty.
Some people do not steal your light. They simply never refill what they consume. The spirits call this a slow theft.
resolutions: Protect your mornings. The first hour of your day belongs to you alone — no messages, no demands, no exceptions.
The ritual has marked the drain. When you leave someone's presence and feel hollow, that emptiness is not yours — it is what they left behind. Recognize it. Do not carry it.
You do not owe access to everyone who asks. The ritual draws a line in the ash — cross it only for those who replenish what they take.
prophecies: The cord between you and this person is dissolving. By the next moon, you will feel lighter without knowing why.
Your energy will return — not slowly, but suddenly. One morning you will wake up and recognize yourself again.
The universe compensates those who learn to protect themselves. What you preserve now will multiply.

insights: Their aggression is not strength — it is fear wearing a loud voice. The ritual has seen through it.
The conflict they bring is not random. It is a mirror reflecting something they refuse to face in themselves.
You did not attract this person. But you have been absorbing their chaos as if it belonged to you. It does not.
resolutions: Do not fight on their terms. Silence and distance are weapons they have no defense against.
The ritual advises: protect your peace like a fortress. Let them rage outside the walls.
Their words are loud. Your silence is louder. The ritual advises: let them build their case against themselves. You simply step aside and watch.
prophecies: Their power peaks now. From here, it is all erosion. Watch it happen without lifting a finger.
Peace is approaching your life. Not the absence of conflict — the presence of clarity.
The universe does not ignore cruelty indefinitely. What they built through fear will not survive the shift that is coming.

insights: This person was not sent to destroy you. They were sent to reveal something you needed to see about yourself.
The ritual does not judge your choice. But the spirits note: the intensity of your feelings toward them is a signal, not a sentence.
Whatever this person represents in your life, the ritual has marked it. You now have a choice: carry it, or set it down.
resolutions: Trust what you felt during the ritual. Your instincts are more accurate than your doubts.
The ritual advises: whatever you do next, do not do nothing. Inaction is the only path the curse cannot protect you from.
Face what this person represents, not the person themselves. The real battle was never with them.
prophecies: After this ritual, you will move through the world differently. Not louder — more certain.
The forge does not ask permission. What you survive here will reshape you — not gently, but permanently. You will not recognize the person you become. That is the gift.
The oracle speaks: you are on the edge of something new. This person was the last obstacle before the door opens.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: BeatPetty ("we", "us", "our") respects your privacy. This policy explains what information we collect, how we use it, and your rights.
privacy.infoP1: Names you enter during the ritual are used solely within the ritual experience and are never stored on our servers. They exist only in your browser's temporary session and are gone when you close the page.
privacy.infoP2: Payment processing is handled entirely by Stripe. We do not see, store, or have access to your credit card number, bank details, or full payment credentials. Stripe provides us only with a confirmation of successful payment and the metadata you chose to share (such as the name you entered).
privacy.infoP3: We use Google Analytics 4 to understand how visitors use our site (pages visited, time spent, device type). This data is anonymous and cannot identify you personally.
privacy.cookiesP1: We use minimal cookies: Google Analytics uses cookies to distinguish visitors. No advertising cookies are used. No third-party tracking beyond analytics.
privacy.dataP1: We do not sell, rent, trade, or share any personal data with third parties — period.
privacy.rightsP1: You can request deletion of any data we hold about you by emailing
privacy.changesP1: We may update this policy occasionally. Changes will be posted on this page with a revised "Last updated" date.
privacy.contactP1: For any privacy-related questions, reach us at

refund.title: Refund Policy
refund.intro: We want you to be satisfied. If you're not, here's how refunds work.
refund.guaranteeP1: If you are unsatisfied with your purchase for any reason, we will issue a full refund — no questions asked.
refund.howP2: with the email address you used during checkout and (if available) your order confirmation. We'll process your refund within 3 business days.
refund.processP1: Refunds are issued through Stripe back to your original payment method. Depending on your bank or card issuer, it may take 5–10 business days to appear on your statement.
refund.digitalP1: Because our product is a digital experience, we do not require you to "return" anything. Simply email us and we'll take care of it.

common.privacyNote: Names entered are not stored, shared, or used for any other purpose.
common.privacyFull: Privacy: Any names you enter are used solely within the ritual and are never stored, shared, or used for any other purpose.
